source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A ray of light is incident on a spherical mirror parallel to its principal axis.
Which of the following diagrams shows the reflected ray correctly?
Note: The smooth side is the reflecting part of the mirror, and the dotted side is the back.
<image_1>
extraction_notes: Answer field is option letter A; mapped to option content <image_2>.
Record contains image placeholders and PIL.Image representations, but no explicit image path/URL/file name was provided.

answer_text is an image placeholder ('<image_2>'), but no corresponding image asset/path for image_2 is provided.

all answer choices are image placeholders rather than textual options
multi_step: the task requires interpreting the mirror diagram and applying the reflection rule for a ray parallel to the principal axis
the note about smooth side vs dotted side adds an additional condition to identify the reflecting surface
multiple-choice format with 4 visual options supports some comparison, but this is still a relatively focused single-concept physics item
verifiability: the question is multiple-choice with a finite set of 4 options, so a final answer should in principle be checkable
